member.name: Callum Anderson
member.synopsis: Callum Anderson is the Labour MP for Buckingham and Bletchley , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Nature Conservation: Public Participation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps she is taking to encourage public participation in nature in Buckingham and Bletchley constituency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government is committed to giving everyone in the country the opportunity to engage with nature. As set out in the Environmental Improvement Plan 2025 there are a range of actions being taken at the national level to improve access to nature includin...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Peatlands: Conservation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps she is taking to restore peatlands.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government is committed to peatland restoration. We have announced that we will invest £85 million between 2026 and 2030 to improve and restore our peatlands. This includes extending the Nature for Climate Peatland Grant Scheme by an additional year,...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Nature Conservation: Crime
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps she is taking to tackle wildlife crime in Buckinghamshire.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Wildlife and Countryside Act 1981 protect all wild birds and some wild animals in England. There are strict penalties in place for offences under this and other wildlife legislation. Defra supports the valuable work of the National Wildlife Crime Unit...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Resident Doctors: Training
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of increased resident doctor training places on regional workforce levels, including in underserved and rural areas.
writtenQuestions.answerText: On 29 June 2026, the British Medical Association’s Resident Doctors Committee announced that their membership had voted to accept an offer from the Government to resolve their dispute on pay and jobs.The deal for resident doctors includes the introduction...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Resident Doctors: Training
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to ensure that the planned expansion of resident doctor training places is matched by adequate (i) clinical supervision capacity and (ii) training infrastructure
writtenQuestions.answerText: On 29 June 2026, the British Medical Association’s Resident Doctors Committee announced that their membership had voted to accept an offer from the Government to resolve their dispute on pay and jobs.The deal for resident doctors includes the introduction...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Accident and Emergency Departments
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what discussions he has had with NHS England on the potential impact of the resident doctors’ settlement on staffing in accident and emergency departments.
writtenQuestions.answerText: My Rt Hon. Friend, the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, regularly discusses a range of matters with NHS England.No specific discussions on the impact of the resident doctors’ settlement in England on staffing in accident and emergency depart...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Defence: Manufacturing Industries
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what assessment he has made of the international competitiveness of UK defence manufacturing costs.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Ministry of Defence assesses that UK defence manufacturing remains internationally competitive despite global supply chain pressures and rising costs, with ongoing efforts in innovation, efficiency, and collaboration to sustain its strong global posit...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Defence: Research
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what assessment he has made of the potential for defence investment to increase private sector research intensity in the UK economy.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Defence investment in science and technology has the potential to increase private sector research intensity by providing clear demand signals, co-funding opportunities and technology roadmaps that encourage defence industry to undertake additional resear...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Resident Doctors
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment his Department has made of the potential impact of the new resident doctor pay and training settlement on rates of doctor emigration.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The new resident doctor pay and training settlement in England (the Deal) is made up of measures which address concerns raised by the British Medical Association’s Resident Doctors Committee on behalf of resident doctors. Under the Deal resident doctors i...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Resident Doctors: Pay
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of the resident doctors’ pay settlement on long-term retention rates of doctors within the NHS workforce.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The new resident doctor pay and training settlement in England (the Deal) is made up of measures which address concerns raised by the British Medical Association’s Resident Doctors Committee on behalf of resident doctors. Under the Deal resident doctors i...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Defence: Capital Investment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what assessment he has made of the level of private capital investment required to meet the UK’s defence capability requirements over the next decade.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is making a generational investment in defence. The Defence Investment Plan is backed by £298 billion over the next four years and sets out the major investment choices needed to deliver the Strategic Defence Review. Our ambition is to make...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Defence: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, whether he plans to publish a framework for measuring the economic return on defence investment.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department is committed to ensuring defence is an engine for growth.Defence guarantees our peace and security, which underpins all UK economic growth. The economic return is significant but not amenable to quantification.Additional growth is achieved ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Defence: Supply Chains
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what steps he is taking with Cabinet colleagues to improve productivity growth among firms operating in UK defence supply chains.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Ministry of Defence works closely with other Government Departments, particularly the Department for Business and Trade (DBT), HM Treasury, the Cabinet Office, UK Research and Innovation (UKRI) and the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Defence: Procurement
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what estimate he has made of the proportion of defence procurement spending currently awarded to firms less than five years old.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Ministry of Defence does not hold the information requested.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Defence: Small Businesses
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what steps he is taking with Cabinet colleagues to reduce the cost of capital for early-stage defence firms.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Ministry of Defence works closely with HM Treasury, the Department for Business and Trade, and the British Business Bank to improve access to finance and reduce the cost of capital for early-stage defence firms. This includes strengthening investor co...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Defence: Capital Investment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what estimate he has made of the proportion of defence investment expected to come from domestic rather than overseas private capital by 2030.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Ministry of Defence has not set a fixed target for the proportion of defence investment expected to come from domestic rather than overseas private capital by 2030. The Government’s priority is to make the UK the most attractive place in the world to ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Community Energy: Batteries
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment he has made of barriers to community ownership of battery storage infrastructure.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises that community-owned battery projects can face a range of barriers, including high upfront capital costs, complex grid connection processes, and challenges around onboarding residents. On 4 June, the Department launched a Call fo...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: National Grid
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment he has made of the adequacy of current electricity grid capacity to meet projected demand by 2035.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The National Energy System Operator (NESO) conducts independent modelling of future electricity demand and supply and strategic planning of the transmission network. NESO’s Future Energy Scenarios (FES) provide a range of credible pathways for Great Brita...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: National Grid: Infrastructure
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what steps his Department is taking to accelerate private capital investment in grid infrastructure.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Ofgem regulates network companies to ensure efficient investment in maintaining and upgrading electricity infrastructure. The Department works with Ofgem to ensure the regulatory framework supports timely electricity network investment. Ofgem’s RIIO price...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Mental Health Services: Buckingham and Bletchley
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of trends in the level of waiting times for NHS Talking Therapies in Buckingham and Bletchley constituency since July 2024.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department monitors waiting times for NHS Talking Therapies through published monthly statistics and information provided by NHS England and local providers.For Buckinghamshire Talking Therapies, which covers Buckingham, there has been no clear overal...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care
